text:
There lived a wise old turtle who visited every pond in the forest every week. One day, a curious young fox asked him why he kept visiting all those ponds.

The old turtle smiled sadly. "I collect stones," he explained. "Each pond is filled with something unique -- a different rock, a pebble I've touched."

The fox thought for a moment. "Why don't you keep them at home?"

"Because," the turtle said with a soft sigh, "every pond has a story. Each stone carries memories of creatures that once resided there."

The fox nodded thoughtfully. One week later, he saw the turtle at a new pond, collecting stones. The fox approached him.

"Why are you collecting stones today?" he asked.

The turtle paused, then replied, "Now that you mention it, I didn’t realize I was doing it. It’s about remembering—a chance to relive moments."

The fox leaned closer. "You're lucky. That's all you need
